title: Canada vs Australia
shortAnswer: Canada offers cheaper French-language programs and spousal work permits, while Australia provides longer post-study work visas for PhD holders. Both have similar costs otherwise.

keyDifferences.label: Spousal work permits
keyDifferences.entityAValue: Open work permits for graduate students' spouses

prosA: Extremely affordable French-language programs in Quebec
Spousal open work permits for graduate students
Significant cost savings in smaller cities like Montreal and Ottawa
Post-study work permit matches full study duration
consA: Shorter maximum post-study work visa compared to Australia
Limited to 3 years work permit even for PhD holders
prosB: Longer post-study work opportunities for PhD holders (4 years)
Flexible 2-4 year work visa range based on qualifications
Strong career opportunities with low unemployment
consB: No equivalent to Canada's low-cost French programs
No mention of spousal work permit benefits
verdict: Canada wins for students seeking affordable education through French programs and those with spouses needing work permits. Australia is better for PhD holders wanting longer post-study work opportunities and flexible visa durations.
